memoryTip: Sounds like 'sign' — you sign in to say you ARE here.
grammarNote: Irregular: ich bin, du bist, er ist.
usageNote: The most common verb; also the possessive 'his'.

grammarNote: Irregular: ich habe, du hast, er hat.
usageNote: Used to form the perfect tense.

grammarNote: ich werde, du wirst, er wird.
Modal: ich kann, du kannst, er kann.
usageNote: Followed by an infinitive at the end.

grammarNote: Modal: ich muss, du musst, er muss.
usageNote: Obligation; infinitive goes to the end.

grammarNote: ich mache, du machst, er macht.
ich gehe, du gehst, er geht.
ich komme, du kommst, er kommt.
usageNote: 'Woher kommst du?' = Where are you from?

grammarNote: ich sehe, du siehst, er sieht.
ich gebe, du gibst, er gibt.
ich sage, du sagst, er sagt.
Irregular: ich weiß, du weißt, er weiß.
ich finde, du findest, er findet.
ich denke, du denkst, er denkt.

memoryTip: Picture you 'name' the thing you take.
grammarNote: ich nehme, du nimmst, er nimmt.
ich spreche, du sprichst, er spricht.
ich esse, du isst, er isst.
ich trinke, du trinkst, er trinkt.
ich kaufe, du kaufst, er kauft.
ich arbeite, du arbeitest, er arbeitet.

memoryTip: You feel 'at one' (won) where you live.
grammarNote: ich wohne, du wohnst, er wohnt.
ich lerne, du lernst, er lernt.
ich spiele, du spielst, er spielt.
ich fahre, du fährst, er fährt.
ich schlafe, du schläfst, er schläft.
ich helfe, du hilfst, er hilft.

memoryTip: Think: I 'brauch' a break — I need one.
grammarNote: ich brauche, du brauchst, er braucht.
ich verstehe, du verstehst, er versteht.
ich liebe, du liebst, er liebt.
Masculine: der/ein Mann; plural die Männer.

memoryTip: 'Frau' also = 'Mrs', a title for a woman.
grammarNote: Feminine: die/eine Frau; plural die Frauen.
Neuter: das/ein Kind; plural die Kinder.
Neuter: das/ein Haus; plural die Häuser.
Feminine: die/eine Stadt; plural die Städte.
Neuter: das Wasser.
Neuter: das/ein Buch; plural die Bücher.
Masculine: der/ein Tag; plural die Tage.

memoryTip: 'Zeit' sounds like 'tsight' — time flies in sight.
grammarNote: Feminine: die Zeit.
Neuter: das/ein Jahr; plural die Jahre.
Feminine: die Arbeit.
Neuter: das Geld.
Feminine: die/eine Schule; plural die Schulen.
Masculine: der/ein Freund; fem. die Freundin.
Feminine: die/eine Hand; plural die Hände.
Neuter: das/ein Auto; plural die Autos.
Feminine: die Liebe.
Comparative: besser; superlative: am besten.
Comparative: schlechter.
Comparative: größer; superlative: am größten.
Comparative: kleiner.
Comparative: neuer.
Comparative: älter.
Comparative: schöner.
Comparative: wichtiger.

memoryTip: 'Schnell' — say it fast, it sounds quick.
grammarNote: Comparative: schneller.
Comparative: langsamer.
Comparative: glücklicher.
Comparative: müder.
Subject (nominative) pronoun.
Informal singular subject pronoun.
Masculine singular subject pronoun.
Verb form distinguishes she vs they.
First-person plural pronoun.
Negates verbs and adjectives.
Intensifier before adjectives/adverbs.
Adverb of addition.
Adverb of frequency.
Time adverb.
Lowercase = tomorrow; 'der Morgen' = morning.
Time adverb.
Place adverb.
Coordinating conjunction; no word-order change.
Coordinating conjunction.
Coordinating conjunction.
Subordinating: sends the verb to the end.
Subordinating: verb goes to the end.
Two-way: dative (location) or accusative (motion).
Always takes the dative case.

exampleGerman: Das Buch ist auf dem Tisch.
exampleEnglish: The book is on the table.
grammarNote: Two-way preposition.
Always takes the accusative.

exampleEnglish: I am going to your place.
grammarNote: Dative as preposition; also 'too' as adverb.
Affirmative particle.
Negative particle.
Set expression.

memoryTip: Multi-tool word: please AND you're welcome.
grammarNote: Set expression.

exampleEnglish: Excuse me, where is the station?
grammarNote: Noun used as an interjection.
Counting form; as article 'ein/eine'.

exampleEnglish: Shall we go out to eat?
grammarNote: Verb + infinitive set phrase.
Feminine: die Familie.
Adverb; can trigger inversion.
Sentence adverb.
Feminine: die Möglichkeit; pl. -en.
ich entwickle, du entwickelst.

exampleEnglish: I have a lot of experience.
grammarNote: Feminine: die Erfahrung; pl. -en.

memoryTip: Think 'be-shift' — shift your focus onto something.
grammarNote: Often reflexive: 'sich beschäftigen mit'.
Masculine: der Zusammenhang; pl. -hänge.
Feminine: die Voraussetzung; pl. -en.

exampleEnglish: I am buying a new book.
memoryTip: 'Kaufen' sounds like 'cow-fen' — you head to the farm to buy a cow.
grammarNote: Regular weak verb: kaufte, hat gekauft.
usageNote: Very common everyday verb used for all purchases.

memoryTip: 'Ver-kaufen' = the reverse of kaufen (buying). Ver- often signals reversal.
grammarNote: Regular: verkaufte, hat verkauft.

memoryTip: 'Öffnen' — the umlaut ö opens up like a mouth.
grammarNote: Regular: öffnete, hat geöffnet.

memoryTip: 'Schließen' — imagine sliding a sleek lock shut.
grammarNote: Strong verb: schloss, hat geschlossen.
Regular: fragte, hat gefragt.

exampleGerman: Bitte antworten Sie auf meine E-Mail.
grammarNote: Regular: antwortete, hat geantwortet. Takes dative: jemandem antworten.
Strong verb: half, hat geholfen. Takes dative: jemandem helfen.

exampleEnglish: I would like to pay, please.
memoryTip: 'Bezahlen' — 'zahlen' means to count/pay; 'be-' makes it more specific.
grammarNote: Regular: bezahlte, hat bezahlt.

exampleEnglish: We like to travel to Italy.
memoryTip: 'Reisen' — think of 'rising' up to travel somewhere.
grammarNote: Regular: reiste, ist gereist. Uses sein as auxiliary.

exampleEnglish: Can you stay a bit longer?
memoryTip: 'Bleiben' — 'blaib-en', like a label that sticks around.
grammarNote: Strong verb: blieb, ist geblieben. Uses sein as auxiliary.

exampleGerman: Der Film beginnt um acht Uhr.
exampleEnglish: The film starts at eight o'clock.
grammarNote: Strong verb: begann, hat begonnen. Synonym: anfangen (separable).

memoryTip: 'Vergessen' — 'ver-' often signals going wrong; you've let it slip away.
grammarNote: Strong verb: vergaß, hat vergessen.

exampleGerman: Ich erinnere mich an unseren Urlaub.
grammarNote: Reflexive: sich erinnern an + accusative. Regular: erinnerte, hat erinnert.

exampleGerman: Kannst du mir den Weg zeigen?
exampleEnglish: Can you show me the way?
grammarNote: Regular: zeigte, hat gezeigt.
Strong verb: schrieb, hat geschrieben.

memoryTip: 'Wetter' sounds like 'wetter' — rainy weather makes things wetter!
grammarNote: Neuter noun, no plural. Article: das.

memoryTip: 'Familie' is almost identical to the English 'family'.
grammarNote: Feminine noun. Plural: Familien. Article: die.
Neuter noun. Plural: Probleme. Article: das.

exampleGerman: Der Bahnhof ist fünf Minuten entfernt.
exampleEnglish: The train station is five minutes away.
memoryTip: 'Bahn' = rail, 'Hof' = yard. A yard for railways.
grammarNote: Masculine noun. Plural: Bahnhöfe. Article: der.

exampleGerman: Das Flugzeug landet in einer Stunde.
exampleEnglish: The plane lands in one hour.
grammarNote: Neuter noun. Plural: Flugzeuge. Article: das.
Feminine noun. Plural: Straßen. Article: die.

exampleGerman: Mein Zimmer ist groß und hell.
exampleEnglish: My room is large and bright.
grammarNote: Neuter noun. Plural: Zimmer (unchanged). Article: das.

exampleEnglish: I am reading an interesting book.
memoryTip: 'Buch' is related to 'beech' — ancient writing was carved on beech bark.
grammarNote: Neuter noun. Plural: Bücher. Article: das.

memoryTip: 'Küche' — like 'cook-a', the place where cooking happens.
grammarNote: Feminine noun. Plural: Küchen. Article: die.

exampleGerman: Er liest jeden Morgen die Zeitung.
exampleEnglish: He reads the newspaper every morning.
memoryTip: 'Zeit' = time; a newspaper records the times.
grammarNote: Feminine noun. Plural: Zeitungen. Article: die.

exampleGerman: Das Hotel ist billig aber sauber.
exampleEnglish: The hotel is cheap but clean.
memoryTip: 'Billig' — a bill you're happy to pay (a small one).
'Leicht' mirrors 'light' in English — both mean easy and not heavy.
usageNote: Can mean physically light or easy depending on context.
Like leicht, means both physically heavy and mentally difficult.

memoryTip: 'Früh' — sounds like brewing early morning coffee.
'Spät' — sounds like 'spate', a late rush of events.
'Ver-stehen' — 'stehen' = to stand; understanding is standing firm on an idea.
grammarNote: Strong verb: verstand, hat verstanden.

exampleEnglish: I try to be on time.
memoryTip: 'Ver-suchen' — suchen means to seek; you're seeking to achieve something.
grammarNote: Regular: versuchte, hat versucht. Often followed by zu + infinitive.

exampleGerman: Wir haben ein tolles Restaurant entdeckt.
memoryTip: 'Ent-decken' — 'decken' = to cover; ent- removes the cover = discover.
grammarNote: Regular: entdeckte, hat entdeckt.

exampleGerman: Ich habe viel in meinem Leben erlebt.
exampleEnglish: I have experienced a lot in my life.
memoryTip: 'Er-leben' — 'leben' = to live; erleben is to actively live something.
grammarNote: Regular: erlebte, hat erlebt.

memoryTip: 'Gehören' — from 'hören' (to hear); something belongs to whoever heeds it.
grammarNote: Regular: gehörte, hat gehört. Takes dative: Das gehört mir.

exampleGerman: Man sollte Äpfel nicht mit Birnen vergleichen.
exampleEnglish: One shouldn't compare apples with pears.
memoryTip: 'Ver-gleichen' — 'gleich' = equal; to compare is to check for equality.
grammarNote: Strong verb: verglich, hat verglichen.

exampleEnglish: The company is developing new products.
memoryTip: 'Entwickeln' — 'wickeln' = to wrap; develop is to unwrap potential.
grammarNote: Regular: entwickelte, hat entwickelt. Also reflexive: sich entwickeln.

exampleEnglish: Can you explain that to me?
memoryTip: 'Er-klären' — 'klar' = clear; to explain is to make things clear.
grammarNote: Regular: erklärte, hat erklärt.

memoryTip: 'Vor-stellen' — to place (stellen) something in front (vor) of someone.
grammarNote: Separable: stellt vor, vorgestellt. Reflexive: sich vorstellen.
usageNote: 'Sich vorstellen' = to introduce oneself OR to imagine something.

exampleGerman: Ich erwarte eine Antwort bis morgen.
exampleEnglish: I expect an answer by tomorrow.
memoryTip: 'Er-warten' — 'warten' = to wait; to expect is to wait for the anticipated.
grammarNote: Regular: erwartete, hat erwartet.

exampleEnglish: She has a lot of professional experience.
memoryTip: 'Er-fahrung' — 'fahren' = to travel; experience is the journey you've driven.
grammarNote: Feminine noun. Plural: Erfahrungen. Article: die.

memoryTip: 'Möglich' = possible; -keit turns adjectives into nouns (like -ness in English).
grammarNote: Feminine noun. Plural: Möglichkeiten. Article: die.

memoryTip: From 'entscheiden' (to decide). The -ung suffix nominalises the action.
grammarNote: Feminine noun. Plural: Entscheidungen. Article: die.

exampleGerman: Das Ergebnis der Prüfung war gut.
exampleEnglish: The result of the exam was good.
memoryTip: 'Er-geb-nis' — from 'geben' (to give); a result is what the effort gives you.
grammarNote: Neuter noun. Plural: Ergebnisse. Article: das.

exampleGerman: In unserer Gesellschaft ist das normal.
exampleEnglish: In our society that is normal.
memoryTip: 'Geselle' was a journeyman craftsman; a society is a group of companions.
grammarNote: Feminine noun. Plural: Gesellschaften. Article: die.

exampleGerman: Die technische Entwicklung geht schnell voran.
memoryTip: From 'entwickeln' (to develop). -ung makes it a noun.
grammarNote: Feminine noun. Plural: Entwicklungen. Article: die.

exampleGerman: Was ist die Bedeutung dieses Wortes?
exampleEnglish: What is the meaning of this word?
memoryTip: 'Be-deut-ung' — from 'deuten' (to interpret). Meaning is what a word points to.
grammarNote: Feminine noun. Plural: Bedeutungen. Article: die.

memoryTip: 'Be-zieh-ung' — from 'ziehen' (to pull); a relationship pulls people together.
grammarNote: Feminine noun. Plural: Beziehungen. Article: die.

exampleGerman: Was ist der Unterschied zwischen A und B?
exampleEnglish: What is the difference between A and B?
memoryTip: 'Unter-schied' — from 'scheiden' (to separate). The difference cuts below.
grammarNote: Masculine noun. Plural: Unterschiede. Article: der.

exampleGerman: Er trägt die Verantwortung für das Projekt.
exampleEnglish: He bears responsibility for the project.
memoryTip: 'Ver-antwortung' — from 'Antwort' (answer); you must answer for your actions.
grammarNote: Feminine noun. No plural in common use. Article: die.

exampleEnglish: It is necessary to get up early.

memoryTip: 'Möglich' — close to the word for possibility (Möglichkeit).
'Ver-schieden' — from 'scheiden' (to separate); what's separated becomes different.
'Erfolg' = success; '-reich' = rich in. Rich in success = successful.

exampleGerman: Ich bin sehr zufrieden mit meiner Arbeit.
exampleEnglish: I am very satisfied with my work.
memoryTip: 'Zu-frieden' — 'Frieden' = peace; being content is being at peace.

english: to consider / to take into account
exampleEnglish: Please take all factors into account.
memoryTip: 'Rücksicht' = consideration (lit. looking back). To look back at all angles.
grammarNote: Regular: berücksichtigte, hat berücksichtigt. Formal register.

exampleGerman: Die Medien beeinflussen die öffentliche Meinung.
memoryTip: 'Be-einflussen' — 'Einfluss' = influence; to channel a flow into something.
grammarNote: Regular: beeinflusste, hat beeinflusst.

english: to represent / to depict / to portray
exampleGerman: Das Diagramm stellt die Daten dar.
memoryTip: 'Dar-stellen' — 'dar' is an old word for 'there'; to place something there for all to see.
grammarNote: Separable: stellt dar, dargestellt.

exampleEnglish: The task requires a lot of patience.
grammarNote: Regular: erforderte, hat erfordert.

exampleGerman: Wir gewährleisten die Qualität unserer Produkte.
exampleEnglish: We guarantee the quality of our products.
grammarNote: Regular: gewährleistete, hat gewährleistet. Formal register.

exampleGerman: Das ist eine große Herausforderung für uns.
exampleEnglish: That is a big challenge for us.
memoryTip: 'Heraus-forderung' — 'heraus' = out, 'fordern' = demand. A challenge calls you out.
grammarNote: Feminine noun. Plural: Herausforderungen. Article: die.

english: the impact / the effect / the consequence
exampleGerman: Die Auswirkungen des Klimawandels sind spürbar.
exampleEnglish: The effects of climate change are noticeable.
memoryTip: 'Aus-wirkung' — 'wirken' = to work/act; an effect is what works itself out.
grammarNote: Feminine noun. Plural: Auswirkungen. Article: die.

exampleEnglish: Good German skills are a prerequisite.
memoryTip: 'Vor-aus-setzung' — putting something (setzen) out (aus) in front (vor) as a condition.
grammarNote: Feminine noun. Plural: Voraussetzungen. Article: die.

memoryTip: From 'überzeugen' (to convince); a conviction is what has convinced you.
grammarNote: Feminine noun. Plural: Überzeugungen. Article: die.

memoryTip: 'Ein-schränk-ung' — 'Schrank' is a cupboard with fixed boundaries.
grammarNote: Feminine noun. Plural: Einschränkungen. Article: die.

memoryTip: From 'anerkennen' (to recognise); the -ung form captures the act.
grammarNote: Feminine noun. No common plural. Article: die.

memoryTip: 'Ge-legen-heit' — 'gelegen' = conveniently located; an opportunity is conveniently placed for you.
grammarNote: Feminine noun. Plural: Gelegenheiten. Article: die.

memoryTip: 'Wesen-tlich' — 'Wesen' = essence/being; essential is of the essence.
'Er-heb-lich' — 'heben' = to lift; erheblich raises the level of something.
'Viel-fältig' — 'viel' = many, 'fältig' = folded. Many folds = many varieties.
'Nach-haltig' — 'nachhalten' = to echo; sustainable things keep echoing on.
'Zu-verlässig' — from 'verlassen' (to rely on); someone you can leave things to.

exampleEnglish: It is based on mutual respect.
memoryTip: 'Gegen-seitig' — 'gegen' = facing, 'Seite' = side. Facing each other on both sides.
'Un-ge-fähr' — 'Gefahr' = danger; an estimate keeps you out of the danger of being exact.

exampleGerman: Ich gehe jeden Tag zur Arbeit.
exampleEnglish: I go to work every day.
memoryTip: 'Arbeit' — think 'hard-bite' — work you have to bite into.
grammarNote: Feminine noun. Plural: Arbeiten. Article: die.
Masculine noun. Plural: Freunde. Article: der.

exampleEnglish: I am meeting my friend tomorrow.
grammarNote: Feminine noun. Plural: Freundinnen. Article: die.

memoryTip: 'Geld' — related to 'gold'; money was once measured in gold.
grammarNote: Neuter noun. No plural for the general concept. Article: das.

exampleEnglish: I have to go to the doctor.
grammarNote: Masculine noun. Plural: Ärzte. Feminine: Ärztin. Article: der.

memoryTip: 'Kranken-haus' — 'krank' = sick, 'Haus' = house. A house for sick people.
grammarNote: Neuter noun. Plural: Krankenhäuser. Article: das.
Feminine noun. Plural: Schulen. Article: die.
Masculine noun. Plural: Lehrer. Feminine: Lehrerin. Article: der.

exampleGerman: Der Zug fährt um zehn Uhr ab.
exampleEnglish: The train departs at ten o'clock.
memoryTip: 'Zug' also means 'pull' — a train pulls carriages behind it.
grammarNote: Masculine noun. Plural: Züge. Article: der.

memoryTip: 'Auto' comes from Greek 'autos' (self), as in automobile.
grammarNote: Neuter noun. Plural: Autos. Article: das.

exampleGerman: Meine Wohnung ist im dritten Stock.
exampleEnglish: My apartment is on the third floor.
memoryTip: 'Wohn-ung' from 'wohnen' (to live/reside). The place where you reside.
grammarNote: Feminine noun. Plural: Wohnungen. Article: die.

exampleGerman: Ich laufe jeden Morgen im Park.
exampleEnglish: I run in the park every morning.
memoryTip: 'Laufen' — think of 'loping' along at a run.
grammarNote: Strong verb: lief, ist gelaufen. Uses sein as auxiliary.
usageNote: In everyday speech, 'laufen' often just means to walk.

exampleGerman: Wir treffen uns um drei Uhr.
exampleEnglish: We are meeting at three o'clock.
grammarNote: Strong verb: traf, hat getroffen. Reflexive: sich treffen = to meet each other.

memoryTip: 'Kochen' — sounds like 'cook-en'; the -en is just the German infinitive ending.
grammarNote: Regular: kochte, hat gekocht.

exampleEnglish: Health is the most important thing.
memoryTip: 'Ge-sund-heit' — 'gesund' = healthy; -heit makes it a noun. Also what Germans say when you sneeze!
grammarNote: Feminine noun. No plural. Article: die.

memoryTip: 'Um-welt' — 'um' = around, 'Welt' = world. The world around you.
grammarNote: Feminine noun. No plural. Article: die.

memoryTip: 'Sprache' — from 'sprechen' (to speak); a language is the system of speech.
grammarNote: Feminine noun. Plural: Sprachen. Article: die.

memoryTip: 'Wirt' is a host who manages resources — 'Wirtschaft' = the art of managing.
grammarNote: Feminine noun. Plural: Wirtschaften. Article: die.

english: the reason / the ground / the basis
memoryTip: 'Grund' = ground. A reason is the ground on which an argument stands.
grammarNote: Masculine noun. Plural: Gründe. Article: der.
usageNote: 'Aus diesem Grund' = for this reason. Also literally means ground/bottom.

exampleGerman: Obwohl es regnet, gehe ich spazieren.
exampleEnglish: Although it is raining, I am going for a walk.
memoryTip: 'Ob-wohl' — 'wohl' = well; concedes something seems fine but...
grammarNote: Subordinating conjunction: sends verb to end of clause.

exampleGerman: Es regnet, trotzdem gehe ich aus.
exampleEnglish: It is raining; I am going out nevertheless.
memoryTip: 'Trotz-dem' — 'Trotz' = defiance; you're going ahead despite everything.
'Aller-dings' — 'aller Dinge' = 'of all things'; introduces a qualification.

english: because of / due to / on account of
exampleGerman: Aufgrund des schlechten Wetters bleiben wir zu Hause.
exampleEnglish: Because of the bad weather we are staying home.
memoryTip: 'Auf-grund' — literally 'on the basis of'; on the ground of a reason.
grammarNote: Followed by genitive: aufgrund des Problems.
